---
subtitle: "A Nine-Block Framework Bridging Data and Design"
abstract: |
  This chapter introduces the book's central methodological contribution -- extending the ITU Digital Innovation Toolkit into the nine-block AI-SDG EcoInnovation Canvas (full spec in @sec-app-canvas).
keywords:
  - ITU Digital Innovation Toolkit
  - ecosystem diagnosis
  - canvas methodology
  - technology roadmapping
---

# The ITU-Inspired EcoInnovation Canvas {#sec-itu-ecoinnovation-canvas}

Part I established what the data shows; Part II asks what to design in response. This chapter extends the ITU Digital Innovation Toolkit's *Rapid Ecosystem Diagnosis* and *Flagship Project Co-Creation* phases into a single nine-block canvas, so that each block of the canvas maps directly onto one chapter of this book.

The canvas is deliberately a working artefact, not a finished model: it is meant to be filled in, argued with, and revised as new evidence from Part I arrives.

## From Retrospective Science Mapping to Prospective Service Design
- Why traditional bibliometrics fail without a design layer.

## Extending the ITU Digital Innovation Toolkit
- Mapping onto *Rapid Ecosystem Diagnosis* and *Flagship Project Co-Creation* phases specifically.

## The Nine-Block AI-SDG EcoInnovation Canvas
- Walk-through of the canvas, block by block, with forward references to where each block is operationalized (Blocks 1--3 in Part I, Blocks 4--6 in this Part, Blocks 7--9 in Part III).
- Full machine-readable specification: @sec-app-canvas.

## Method and Data Matrix
- The "Method" row (AI-enabled scientometric roadmapping) and "Data" row (triangulated multi-database horizon data) as a single institutional workflow, for expert and institutional application.

::: {.callout-tip title="Bridge to Chapter 5"}
Chapter 5 operationalizes the canvas into stakeholder-facing service journeys.
:::
